home.title: RegenTwin
home.subtitle: Tissue Regeneration Digital Twin
home.description: Multiscale wound healing simulation platform integrating SDE, ABM, and therapeutic protocols

upload.title: Data Upload
upload.dropzone: Drop .fcs file here or click to select

model.title: Model Selection
model.extendedDesc: Full stochastic model with all cell populations
model.abmDesc: Spatial discrete agents with individual behaviors

export.title: Export
export.unavailable: Export is unavailable for this result

states.unavailableDescription: The simulation metadata is incomplete or the result bundle is missing.

analysis.title: Analysis
analysis.loadedSimulationHint: Parameters were loaded from a completed simulation. You can now run sensitivity analysis on this configuration.

sensitivity.title: Sensitivity
sensitivity.hint: Sensitivity analysis uses the current simulation configuration as a forward model — no data upload is required.
sensitivity.intro: Which model parameters most affect the result? The method runs the simulation N times, varying selected coefficients, and quantifies each contribution.

estimation.title: Parameter Estimation
estimation.needUpload: Upload a time-series CSV to run parameter estimation.
estimation.dataFormatHint: Requires a CSV with a `time` column (hours) and a target variable column (e.g. F, Ne, M1). FCS files contain a single snapshot and are only usable as initial conditions for simulation.
estimation.intro: Fits model parameters to your experimental data. Returns point estimates and confidence intervals for each coefficient.

validation.title: Validation
validation.intro: Compares model behavior against reference datasets from the literature. Helps confirm that the current parameterization reproduces known experiments.
validation.noResult: Run validation to see the result.

sensitivity.title: What is sensitivity analysis
sensitivity.body: Global analysis estimates how strongly the model output (e.g. final fibroblast count) depends on each parameter. High sensitivity = the parameter is critical and must be measured precisely. Low = the model is robust to it.

sobol.title: Sobol method
sobol.body: Variance-based: decomposes output variance into per-parameter contributions. Returns S1 (the parameter alone) and ST (full contribution including interactions). Accurate but expensive: typically n_samples >= 256 for <=10 parameters.

morris.title: Morris (Elementary Effects)
morris.body: Screening method: quickly filters out non-influential parameters. mu* — mean effect magnitude, sigma — nonlinearity/interactions. Suitable for initial screening of 20+ parameters at n_samples = 64-128.

estimation.title: What is parameter estimation
estimation.body: Inverse problem: given a time series of observations, find model parameters that produce the trajectory closest to the data. Returns point estimates and confidence intervals.

mcmc.title: MCMC (Markov Chain Monte Carlo)
mcmc.body: Bayesian sampling of the parameter posterior. Provides full uncertainty (posterior + convergence diagnostics R-hat, ESS). Slow — n_samples 500-2000.

optimization.title: Optimization (MLE)
optimization.body: Maximum Likelihood: finds parameters that maximize likelihood. Fast, but yields only point estimates and approximate CIs. Use when speed matters.

validation.title: What is validation
validation.body: Runs the model on 4 reference scenarios from the literature and compares results to published data. Returns an aggregated score (0-1, higher is better) and error metrics. This is calibration verification, not optimization.

nSamples.title: Number of samples (n_samples)
nSamples.body: Sobol: 256-1024 (grows fast). Morris: 64-256. MCMC: 500-2000 per chain. More = more accurate but slower.

targetVariable.title: Target variable
targetVariable.body: Which model variable to fit to your data. F — fibroblasts, Ne — neutrophils, M1/M2 — macrophages, P — platelets, E — endothelium.

literature-xue2009.name: Xue 2009
literature-xue2009.desc: Fibroblast dynamics in a rat wound model (3-21 days). Classical benchmark.

literature-flegg2010.name: Flegg 2010
literature-flegg2010.desc: Macrophage-angiogenesis interaction in hypoxic wounds. PDE model.

HPA-skin-baseline.name: HPA Skin Baseline
HPA-skin-baseline.desc: Reference cellular concentrations in human skin (Human Protein Atlas). Starting point.

GSE28914.name: GSE28914
GSE28914.desc: Bulk RNA-seq of chronic diabetic wounds. Cytokine expression profile.

actions.selectAtLeastOne: Select at least one parameter to run

about.title: About RegenTwin
about.subtitle: A software tool for multiscale tissue regeneration simulation using flow cytometry data
about.mathModelDesc: The system uses a two-level modeling architecture.
about.macroLevelDesc: Stochastic Differential Equations (Langevin equation) for cell population dynamics with logistic growth, therapeutic factors, and stochastic noise.
about.microLevelDesc: Discrete agents of 6 types with spatial movement, chemotaxis, division, death, and intercellular interactions.
about.prpDesc: Multi-component release kinetics of PDGF, VEGF, TGF-β, EGF with different time constants.
about.pemfDesc: Frequency-dependent effect on proliferation, anti-inflammatory action, and cell migration.
about.synergyDesc: Multiplicative factor for combined application of both methods.
